format: Manual disclosure of every remote/network call, per QVAC hackathon rules.

non_ai_remote_calls.name: Apache LICENSE fetch
non_ai_remote_calls.purpose: one-time build-time download of license text

non_ai_remote_calls.name: Model weights download (Hugging Face)
non_ai_remote_calls.purpose: one-time setup download of MedPsy / OCR / embedding weights; NOT called at inference time

non_ai_remote_calls.name: npm registry
non_ai_remote_calls.purpose: one-time build-time dependency install (@qvac/sdk and native addons)

non_ai_remote_calls.name: React Native prebuilt artifacts (Maven Central)
non_ai_remote_calls.purpose: one-time iOS build-time download of RN 0.85 prebuilt core/hermes/dependency tarballs during pod install; NOT called at runtime

non_ai_remote_calls.name: CocoaPods CDN / Homebrew (build host only)
non_ai_remote_calls.purpose: one-time macOS build-host toolchain/pod setup; never part of the app or runtime

p2p_note: Phone<->laptop delegated inference uses the QVAC SDK's Hyperswarm DHT transport between the user's OWN devices. Peer discovery/NAT traversal use DHT bootstrap nodes + blind relays (blind relays are end-to-end encrypted passthrough by design and cannot read prompts/responses). Inference data flows only between the user's own phone and laptop. (Confirm relay encryption specifics before final submission.)
summary: All AI inference (LLM, OCR, embeddings, RAG, optional TTS/STT) runs locally via @qvac/sdk. Zero runtime remote API calls. Core flow runs in airplane mode.
